caption:
"All are welcome! UIC Accessibility Audit Stakeholder Engagement Meetings 3/13 and 3/17
 
UIC’s Office Planning, Sustainability and Project Management will be engaging with LCM Architects to undergo a massive accessibility survey of all UIC campus spaces. In preparation, they are holding two stakeholder engagement meetings for all members of the UIC community. Stakeholder feedback will help:
 
·  Identify campus accessibility goals
·  Evaluate accessibility standards
·  Rank and organize priorities
·  Establish guidelines for a full campus audit
 
Please attend one of the sessions if you are able!
 
Monday, March 13, 1 PM – 2:30 PM. Join the March 13 meeting using this direct Zoom link, https://uic.zoom.us/j/84743387874?pwd=Wnova2ZYMnJneHdQVWdHUzdwYWZ5Zz09
Friday, March 17, 11 AM – 12:30 PM. Join the March 17 meeting using this direct Zoom link, https://uic.zoom.us/j/89349708612?pwd=T2FiZ2hUamJFUlN3cHh5a2pSZlViZz09

ID: Image is a white notecard with little red dots and all the text listed above is in the image.".

caption:
"Happy New Year! The Spring 2023 semester is quickly approaching and to help you get ready, the DRC will be hosting a "How to Schedule on Accommodate" office hours. These info sessions will be hosted hybrid with some in-person test scheduling office hours during the first 2-weeks of classes. The dates & times are listed below and on the attached flyer. Please make sure to bring in all your Syllabi and get ready to schedule all your tests/quizzes in one seating. 

Please also bring your laptop/tablet. All office hours are open and walk-ins welcome.

caption:
"UIC College of Nursing PhD student Sabrina Jamal-Eddine was selected for the 2022 Humanities Without Walls Predoctoral Career Diversity Summer Workshop.
Humanities Without Walls based at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign’s Humanities Research Institute, is a consortium of humanities centers and institutes at 16 major research universities, including UIC. It is funded by The Andrew W. Mellon Foundation.
In summer 2022, the University of Michigan’s Institute for Humanities will host the annual HWW summer workshop for doctoral students interested in learning about diverse careers as humanists.

Jamal-Eddine will be the first participant to represent the field of nursing since the fellowship program was launched in 2015 and the first applicant accepted from UIC since 2019.

Jamal-Eddine is studying the use of spoken word poetry as critical narrative pedagogy to educate nursing students about ableism and disability justice in health care. Read a profile of Jamal-Eddine to learn how her own experiences as a disabled student informed her research.
She is as a graduate student representative for the UIC Chancellor’s Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities. She is also creative director for the Middle Eastern North African Student Association, which focuses on health care equity for MENA populations, and the nursing graduate student senate representative. In 2019, she performed a TEDx spoken word at The University of Ohio on her experience with Islamophobia and xenophobia.

ID: UIC Nursing PhD student Sabrina Jamal-Eddine delivers her 2019 TEDx spoken word presentation at The University of Ohio on her experience with Islamophobia and xenophobia.".

caption:
"Talila " TL " Lewis
Abolition & Disability Justice
Thurs March 31, 3:30-5:00pm
RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/DisabilityJustice
Hosted by the @uic.dcc & the @aaccatuic, Co-sponsored by the Chicagoland Disabled People of Color Coalition and National Black Deaf Advocates, Inc. (NBDA).

Access info: ASL & CART Provided. Contact dcc@uic.edu/ 312-355-7050 with any other access requests.

Flyer description: Light blue background with a medium blue square and a photo of Talila “TL” Lewis, a Black person with short cropped hair, glasses, a white shirt, and black blazer. Bold gold text at the top says “Talila ‘TL’ Lewis,” and the access info, talk title and details are below in blue, purple, and gold text. In the lower left are logos for the UIC Disability Cultural Center and the UIC African American Cultural Center, and in the lower right is purple text with the co-sponsors, the Chicagoland Disabled People of Color Coalition and National Black Deaf Advocates.

Additional details:
Talila "TL" Lewis (@talilalewis) is a thought leader, community lawyer and organizer who connects disability justice, racial justice, and abolition.

We are looking forward to TL's presentation and thinking together about those connections as TL explains how ableism is at the heart of every form of oppression. TL will also share about how HEARD, a cross-disability advocacy organization that works with deaf/disabled incarcerated people, grew into a firmly abolitionist organization. In hosting this event, we hope to continue UIC's ongoing conversations about intersectional organizing and dreaming radically different futures.
[Read TL's full bio at https://www.talilalewis.com/about.html]".

caption:
"13th Annual UIC Minority Health Conference. 

Call For Abstracts. 

The 2022 UIC Minority Health Conference Planning Team would like to invite you to present your research at this year's conference on April 8th 2022 at 9am CST! Abstracts should relate to this year’s theme, Collective Trauma: The Impact of Changing Community Structure on Health Equity. Suggested topics include industry and the occupation environment, built environment, environmental Justice, healthcare practice, health and illness, mental and emotional health, community activism, race, gender and gender identity, gender-based violence, sexual orientation, prison system, religion, immigration, and COVID-19. Abstract submissions are due February 28, 2022 at 11:59pm CST via Google form. Please direct questions regarding the submission process to info.uic.mhc@gmail.com. We look forward to receiving your submission!  Image Description: White poster with red, white, black, and blue boarder that has all the information stated above. Also has a logo of the UIC Minority Health Conference.".

caption:
"USN is here to help! We are a student initiative working under the UIC Counseling Center that focuses on building strong mental health support by providing support groups and events for the UIC community. 

Currently, we run support groups on Mondays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Tuesdays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Wednesdays at 1 PM or 2 PM, and Fridays at 12 PM. Each group would meet once a week for an hour session that may be held online/in-person. 

Instagram: @usn.uic
Website: https://www.unitedsupportnetwork.org

The link to sign up is here if you do want to advertise for us: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdc68qkhSrgkWRzkbXJHVFgQw2HnOB8_IPd-mWEQ0nQfTHXrA/viewform

ID: Image has green leaves with a soft pink silhouette of people hanging out. The background is a dark forest green. The text reads: United Support Network
Students helping Students

Join a support group for fall
Support group times:
Monday at 11am or 5pm
Tuesday at 11am or 5pm
Wednesday at 1pm or 2pm
Friday at 12pm

Because life happens.".

caption:
"Unlearn to Relearn:
Shifting Attitudes for Arabs with Disabilities 

November 16th from 12:00 to 1:00 pm CST
Disability? Disabled? Special Needs? What language and frameworks do/should we use in the Arab Community?

Join the Arab American Cultural Center and the Disability Cultural Center for a discussion on disability in the Arab/American Community

Featuring:

- Adnan Alaboudi, International Disability Self Advocate, Jordan
- Marina Salman, Access Living Chicago
- Randa Abdelrahim, PhD Candidate UIC

Realtime live captioning (CART) will be provided.

Flyer Description: Light blue background with black arabesque motif constitute the background of the flyer. The Center is covered by a lighter blue sheet with writing in black and read detailing title of event and other information about the event. The logos of the two centers in red are on the bottom right and left.

For questions and/or accessibility, contact us at arabamcc@uic.edu".

caption:
"Calling for new members for the CCSPD on the school year 2021-2022 ! (Chancellor's Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities)

Deadline: Friday, April 30, 2021

The Chancellor's Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities (CCSPD) seeks new members for the upcoming academic year, August 2021 to July 2022. 
 
Our Mission is to promote empowerment and inclusion of disabled students, faculty, and staff at UIC. The Committee recognizes that disabled people/people with disabilities are a diverse group, including individuals with visible and non-visible disabilities. Our work is motivated by the belief that disabled people are assets to the university. 
 
•  CCSPD advises the Chancellor and other administrative units and makes recommendations on disability-related issues, policies, services, attitudes, and practices on campus.
• CCSPD provides a forum for the discussion and assessment of disability-related issues relevant to the campus and the larger community.
 
We are seeking candidates with an eagerness to promote the mission of our committee coupled with a strong desire to learn from others. All members of the UIC Community are encouraged to apply: Undergraduate Students, Graduate Students, Faculty, and Staff
 
If you are interested in serving on the CCSPD, send an email message describing:
 
• why you would like to be a part of the CCSPD
• your skills, background
• how disability-related experiences have shaped your desire to get involved
 
This is a UIC Chancellor appointment and work-approved activity. UIC faculty and staff appointed to the committee are expected to attend our monthly committee meetings. Members are also expected to participate in at least one CCSPD subcommittee. 
 
Please email your cover letter to:
Alejandro Castro (acastr1@uic.edu) and Jennifer Jackson (jmjacksn@uic.edu).
Please include "CCSPD Membership" in the subject line. Deadline: As soon as possible. 

ID: Purple to blue vertical flyer with call for membership description written on white text".

•  CCSPD advises the Chancellor and other administrative units and makes recommendations on disability-related issues, policies, services, attitudes, and practices on campus.
• CCSPD provides a forum for the discussion and assessment of disability-related issues relevant to the campus and the larger community.
 
We are seeking candidates with an eagerness to promote the mission of our committee coupled with a strong desire to learn from others. All members of the UIC Community are encouraged to apply: Undergraduate Students, Graduate Students, Faculty, and Staff
 
If you are interested in serving on the CCSPD, send an email message describing:
 
• why you would like to be a part of the CCSPD
• your skills, background
• how disability-related experiences have shaped your desire to get involved
 
This is a UIC Chancellor appointment and work-approved activity. UIC faculty and staff appointed to the committee are expected to attend our monthly committee meetings. Members are also expected to participate in at least one CCSPD subcommittee. 
 
Please email your cover letter to:
Alejandro Castro (acastr1@uic.edu) and Jennifer Jackson (jmjacksn@uic.edu).
Please include "CCSPD Membership" in the subject line. Deadline: As soon as possible. 

ID: Purple to blue vertical flyer with call for membership description written on white text".

"Check Out This Great Resource! The UIC Disability Cultural Center and the UIC Disability Resource Center have teamed up again to drop a *new accessibility resource*: Captions for Online Classes and Events! UIC Zoom accounts added auto-transcription this semester: so what is the difference between auto-transcription & CART? Who benefits from captions, and how can meeting hosts set it up? This info & more at go.uic.edu/LiveCaptions

caption:
"Do you have a disability and are unsure when and/or how to disclose it during your job search? Need advice on how to enhance your job search using Handshake? If yes, join us TOMORROW on March 16, 12-1PM CST! Presented by UIC Career Services, Disability Cultural Center, Disability Resource Center, and Disability and Human Development

RSVP at: http://go.uic.edu/disclosure

This webinar will help you:
- Get tips on how to maximize your Handshake profile, register for virtual career fairs (such as the upcoming March 18 All Majors Career Fair), and sign up for career fair sessions with recruiters.
- Learn best practices for presenting your disability identity during the job search/hiring process.
- Learn what hiring managers are looking for in job candidates
- Get advice on how to ask employers questions about their employment inclusion policies and practices

CART live captioning will be provided. For any other access needs, please contact shami3@uic.edu or (312) 996-8332.

ID: Circular cut picture of several students huddled together with writing materials on a vertical red flyer of multiple shapes and shades. On top is a red horizontal banner with event title text "Disability Disclosure During the Job Search" on yellow all-caps, and event sponsors/dates on white and blue text. Beside the picture, event details are written with white text on a light pink box with white borders.".
